title: Choose by branching
text: want standard group comparison, stay page. need adjustment, survival analysis, agreement, equivalence, statsguider stop redirect .

title: Scenario tutorials
code: tbl_continuous <- data.frame(   group = c(rep("control", 6), rep("treated", 6)),   biomarker = c(10.2, 10.4, 10.1, 10.5, 10.3, 10.0, 11.1, 11.4, 11.0, 11.3, 11.5, 11.2) )  tbl_continuous #>      group biomarker #> 1  control      10.2 #> 2  control      10.4 #> 3  control      10.1 #> 4  control      10.5 #> 5  control      10.3 #> 6  control      10.0 #> 7  treated      11.1 #> 8  treated      11.4 #> 9  treated      11.0 #> 10 treated      11.3 #> 11 treated      11.5 #> 12 treated      11.2 select_test(   data = tbl_continuous,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   run = "recommend",   language = "en" ) #> statsguider decision #> - Action: recommend #> - Recommended method: Welch t-test #> - Alternative: Mann-Whitney U test #> - Reason: The data look like two independent groups with a continuous outcome and acceptable normality. #> - Next step: Run Welch t-test. Use the rank-based alternative if needed. #> - Notes: #>   * Normality was checked automatically and classified as `yes`. run_test(   data = tbl_continuous,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   language = "en" ) #> statsguider result #> - Method: Welch t-test #> - Reason: The data look like two independent groups with a continuous outcome and acceptable normality. #> - Summary: Welch t-test was selected because the data looked like continuous outcome, 2 groups, paired = no, repeated = no.

title: Scenario tutorials
code: tbl_paired <- data.frame(   id = rep(paste0("S", 1:6), each = 2),   visit = rep(c("before", "after"), times = 6),   value = c(8.2, 9.0, 7.9, 8.8, 8.5, 9.1, 8.1, 8.9, 8.3, 9.0, 8.0, 8.7) )  tbl_paired #>    id  visit value #> 1  S1 before   8.2 #> 2  S1  after   9.0 #> 3  S2 before   7.9 #> 4  S2  after   8.8 #> 5  S3 before   8.5 #> 6  S3  after   9.1 #> 7  S4 before   8.1 #> 8  S4  after   8.9 #> 9  S5 before   8.3 #> 10 S5  after   9.0 #> 11 S6 before   8.0 #> 12 S6  after   8.7 select_test(   data = tbl_paired,   outcome = "value",   group = "visit",   id = "id",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "yes",   repeated = "no",   run = "recommend",   language = "en" ) #> statsguider decision #> - Action: recommend #> - Recommended method: Paired t-test #> - Alternative: Wilcoxon signed-rank test #> - Reason: The data look like two paired measurements with acceptable normality. #> - Next step: Run paired t-test. #> - Notes: #>   * Normality was checked automatically and classified as `yes`.

title: Scenario tutorials
code: tbl_three_groups <- data.frame(   group = rep(c("control", "drugA", "drugB"), each = 5),   biomarker = c(     9.8, 10.1, 10.0, 9.9, 10.2,     10.8, 10.9, 11.1, 10.7, 11.0,     11.4, 11.2, 11.5, 11.3, 11.6   ) )  tbl_three_groups #>      group biomarker #> 1  control       9.8 #> 2  control      10.1 #> 3  control      10.0 #> 4  control       9.9 #> 5  control      10.2 #> 6    drugA      10.8 #> 7    drugA      10.9 #> 8    drugA      11.1 #> 9    drugA      10.7 #> 10   drugA      11.0 #> 11   drugB      11.4 #> 12   drugB      11.2 #> 13   drugB      11.5 #> 14   drugB      11.3 #> 15   drugB      11.6 select_test(   data = tbl_three_groups,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   run = "recommend",   language = "en" ) #> statsguider decision #> - Action: recommend #> - Recommended method: Welch ANOVA #> - Alternative: Kruskal-Wallis test #> - Reason: The data look like three or more independent groups with a continuous outcome and acceptable normality. #> - Next step: Run Welch ANOVA. #> - Notes: #>   * Normality was checked automatically and classified as `yes`.

title: Scenario tutorials
code: tbl_small_cat <- data.frame(   group = c(rep("control", 6), rep("treated", 6)),   response = c("yes", "no", "no", "no", "no", "no", "yes", "yes", "no", "no", "no", "no") )  tbl_small_cat #>      group response #> 1  control      yes #> 2  control       no #> 3  control       no #> 4  control       no #> 5  control       no #> 6  control       no #> 7  treated      yes #> 8  treated      yes #> 9  treated       no #> 10 treated       no #> 11 treated       no #> 12 treated       no select_test(   data = tbl_small_cat,   outcome = "response",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "binary",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   run = "recommend",   language = "en" ) #> statsguider decision #> - Action: recommend #> - Recommended method: Fisher's exact test #> - Alternative: Chi-squared test #> - Reason: The data look like two independent groups with small expected cell counts. #> - Next step: Run Fisher's exact test.

title: Scenario tutorials
code: tbl_paired_binary <- data.frame(   id = rep(paste0("P", 1:8), each = 2),   visit = rep(c("before", "after"), times = 8),   response = c("no", "yes", "no", "no", "yes", "yes", "no", "yes",                "yes", "yes", "no", "yes", "no", "no", "yes", "yes") )  tbl_paired_binary #>    id  visit response #> 1  P1 before       no #> 2  P1  after      yes #> 3  P2 before       no #> 4  P2  after       no #> 5  P3 before      yes #> 6  P3  after      yes #> 7  P4 before       no #> 8  P4  after      yes #> 9  P5 before      yes #> 10 P5  after      yes #> 11 P6 before       no #> 12 P6  after      yes #> 13 P7 before       no #> 14 P7  after       no #> 15 P8 before      yes #> 16 P8  after      yes select_test(   data = tbl_paired_binary,   outcome = "response",   group = "visit",   id = "id",   outcome_type = "binary",   paired = "yes",   repeated = "no",   run = "recommend",   language = "en" ) #> statsguider decision #> - Action: recommend #> - Recommended method: McNemar test #> - Reason: The data look like paired binary measurements. #> - Next step: Run McNemar test.

title: Scenario tutorials
code: tbl_repeated_ordinal <- data.frame(   id = rep(paste0("S", 1:5), each = 3),   visit = rep(c("baseline", "week2", "week4"), times = 5),   score = ordered(     c(       "high", "medium", "low",       "medium", "medium", "low",       "high", "medium", "medium",       "medium", "low", "low",       "high", "medium", "low"     ),     levels = c("low", "medium", "high")   ) )  tbl_repeated_ordinal #>    id    visit  score #> 1  S1 baseline   high #> 2  S1    week2 medium #> 3  S1    week4    low #> 4  S2 baseline medium #> 5  S2    week2 medium #> 6  S2    week4    low #> 7  S3 baseline   high #> 8  S3    week2 medium #> 9  S3    week4 medium #> 10 S4 baseline medium #> 11 S4    week2    low #> 12 S4    week4    low #> 13 S5 baseline   high #> 14 S5    week2 medium #> 15 S5    week4    low select_test(   data = tbl_repeated_ordinal,   outcome = "score",   group = "visit",   id = "id",   outcome_type = "ordinal",   paired = "no",   repeated = "yes",   run = "recommend",   language = "en" ) #> statsguider decision #> - Action: recommend #> - Recommended method: Friedman test #> - Alternative: Repeated-measures ANOVA #> - Reason: The data look like repeated ordinal measurements. #> - Next step: Run Friedman test.

what: Scenario 7: stop and redirect because adjustment is needed
title: Scenario tutorials
code: tbl_adjust <- data.frame(   group = c(rep("control", 6), rep("treated", 6)),   age = c(41, 45, 39, 48, 42, 44, 37, 40, 38, 43, 39, 41),   biomarker = c(10.2, 10.4, 10.1, 10.5, 10.3, 10.0, 11.1, 11.4, 11.0, 11.3, 11.5, 11.2) )  tbl_adjust #>      group age biomarker #> 1  control  41      10.2 #> 2  control  45      10.4 #> 3  control  39      10.1 #> 4  control  48      10.5 #> 5  control  42      10.3 #> 6  control  44      10.0 #> 7  treated  37      11.1 #> 8  treated  40      11.4 #> 9  treated  38      11.0 #> 10 treated  43      11.3 #> 11 treated  39      11.5 #> 12 treated  41      11.2 select_test(   data = tbl_adjust,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   adjust = "yes",   run = "recommend",   language = "en" ) #> statsguider decision #> - Action: redirect #> - Reason: Adjustment was requested so a simple unadjusted test is not the right main branch. #> - Next step: Move to regression or mixed models. #> - Notes: #>   * Adjustment was requested, so a model-based analysis is more appropriate than a simple test. #>   * Normality was checked automatically and classified as `yes`.

what: Scenario 8: stop and redirect because the outcome is a count
title: Scenario tutorials
code: tbl_count <- data.frame(   group = c(rep("control", 6), rep("treated", 6)),   colonies = c(12, 10, 11, 14, 9, 13, 18, 17, 20, 16, 19, 18) )  tbl_count #>      group colonies #> 1  control       12 #> 2  control       10 #> 3  control       11 #> 4  control       14 #> 5  control        9 #> 6  control       13 #> 7  treated       18 #> 8  treated       17 #> 9  treated       20 #> 10 treated       16 #> 11 treated       19 #> 12 treated       18 select_test(   data = tbl_count,   outcome = "colonies",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "count",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   run = "recommend",   language = "en" ) #> statsguider decision #> - Action: redirect #> - Reason: Count outcomes should not be forced into a simple comparison test. #> - Next step: Move to Poisson or negative binomial regression. #> - Notes: #>   * Count outcomes should usually move to Poisson or negative binomial regression.

title: Main functions
text: main entry point. Use : already know relevant columns want choose method directly data properties may want run test immediately Important arguments: data: full table outcome: outcome column group: group condition column id: subject ID column paired repeated data goal: kind question outcome_type: continuous, binary, nominal, ordinal, count paired: "yes" "" repeated: "yes" "" adjust: "yes" "" normality: "auto", "yes", "", "unknown" run: "recommend" "run" language: "en" "ja" change run = "run", selected method executed.
code: tbl_select <- data.frame(   group = c(rep("control", 6), rep("treated", 6)),   biomarker = c(10.2, 10.4, 10.1, 10.5, 10.3, 10.0, 11.1, 11.4, 11.0, 11.3, 11.5, 11.2) )  select_test(   data = tbl_select,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   run = "recommend",   language = "en" ) #> statsguider decision #> - Action: recommend #> - Recommended method: Welch t-test #> - Alternative: Mann-Whitney U test #> - Reason: The data look like two independent groups with a continuous outcome and acceptable normality. #> - Next step: Run Welch t-test. Use the rank-based alternative if needed. #> - Notes: #>   * Normality was checked automatically and classified as `yes`.

title: Main functions
text: Use want decision process, step step. asks : want column outcome column group whether data paired whether data repeated whether adjustment needed outcome type whether normality checked automatically Use guided_test() want question--answer interface. Use select_test() want set everything directly.
code: guided_test(   tbl_select,   answers = list(     goal = "difference",     outcome = "biomarker",     group = "group",     paired = "no",     repeated = "no",     adjust = "no",     outcome_type = "continuous",     normality = "auto"   ),   run = "recommend",   language = "en" ) #> statsguider decision #> - Action: recommend #> - Recommended method: Welch t-test #> - Alternative: Mann-Whitney U test #> - Reason: The data look like two independent groups with a continuous outcome and acceptable normality. #> - Next step: Run Welch t-test. Use the rank-based alternative if needed. #> - Notes: #>   * Normality was checked automatically and classified as `yes`.

title: Main functions
text: Use want recommendation . run test. returns: action recommended method alternative reason next step notes warnings useful want inspect choice running anything.
code: recommend_test(   data = tbl_select,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   language = "en" ) #> statsguider decision #> - Action: recommend #> - Recommended method: Welch t-test #> - Alternative: Mann-Whitney U test #> - Reason: The data look like two independent groups with a continuous outcome and acceptable normality. #> - Next step: Run Welch t-test. Use the rank-based alternative if needed. #> - Notes: #>   * Normality was checked automatically and classified as `yes`.

title: Main functions
text: Use want run supported method directly. first calls recommendation engine. branch inappropriate, stops instead forcing analysis. function stricter manually calling test , can redirect.
code: run_test(   data = tbl_select,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   language = "en" ) #> statsguider result #> - Method: Welch t-test #> - Reason: The data look like two independent groups with a continuous outcome and acceptable normality. #> - Summary: Welch t-test was selected because the data looked like continuous outcome, 2 groups, paired = no, repeated = no.

title: Main functions
text: Use analysis want inspect whether design suitable simple test. checks things : whether required columns exist whether enough groups whether id provided paired repeated data whether branch redirected adjustment best function want validate setup first.
code: check_design(   data = tbl_select,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   adjust = "no",   language = "en" ) #> $ok #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $issues #> character(0) #>  #> $warnings #> character(0) #>  #> $inputs #> $inputs$goal #> [1] "difference" #>  #> $inputs$outcome_type #> [1] "continuous" #>  #> $inputs$group_count #> [1] "2" #>  #> $inputs$paired #> [1] "no" #>  #> $inputs$repeated #> [1] "no" #>  #> $inputs$adjust #> [1] "no"

title: Main functions
text: select_test(): best general entry point guided_test(): best question--question use recommend_test(): best seeing method run_test(): best running recommended method check_design(): best validating setup

title: Start here
text: two kinds arguments. Columns table: data: full data.frame outcome: column want analyze group: column defines groups conditions id: subject ID column paired repeated data Choice-style arguments: "difference": compare groups "association": measure association "adjusted_effect": estimate adjusted effect "time_to_event": analyze time--event data "agreement": measure agreement reproducibility "equivalence": test equivalence non-inferiority "continuous": numeric values "binary": yes/outcomes "nominal": unordered categories "ordinal": ordered categories "count": event counts "yes": subject measured twice "": different subjects compared "yes": subject measured three times "": repeated-measures data "yes": covariate adjustment needed "": simple unadjusted comparison enough "auto": let statsguider check automatically "yes": use normal-data branch "": use non-normal branch "unknown": sure "recommend": choose method "run": choose run method "en": English output "ja": Japanese output

what: How to set them in practice
title: Start here
text: Use goal = "difference" ordinary group comparison. Use outcome_type = "continuous" numeric lab values. Use paired = "yes" subject appears twice. Use repeated = "yes" subject appears three visits conditions. Use adjust = "yes" main analysis needs covariates. Use run = "recommend" first want method.

title: Start here
text: example: data = dat: use table outcome = "biomarker": analyze biomarker column group = "group": compare groups group outcome_type = "continuous": outcome numeric paired = "": groups independent repeated = "": repeated-measures data run = "recommend": show recommended method
code: dat <- subset(statsguider::wet_example, visit == "week4")  select_test(   data = dat,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   run = "recommend" ) #> statsguider decision #> - Action: recommend #> - Recommended method: Welch t-test #> - Alternative: Mann-Whitney U test #> - Reason: The data look like two independent groups with a continuous outcome and acceptable normality. #> - Next step: Run Welch t-test. Use the rank-based alternative if needed. #> - Notes: #>   * Normality was checked automatically and classified as `yes`.

title: Start here
text: important change run = "run".
code: select_test(   data = dat,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   run = "run" ) #> statsguider result #> - Method: Welch t-test #> - Reason: The data look like two independent groups with a continuous outcome and acceptable normality. #> - Summary: Welch t-test was selected because the data looked like continuous outcome, 2 groups, paired = no, repeated = no.

title: Start here
text: guided_test() uses logic select_test(). difference interface: select_test(): set arguments directly guided_test(): answer questions one one
code: guided_test(   dat,   answers = list(     goal = "difference",     outcome = "biomarker",     group = "group",     paired = "no",     repeated = "no",     adjust = "no",     outcome_type = "continuous",     normality = "auto"   ),   language = "en" ) #> statsguider decision #> - Action: recommend #> - Recommended method: Welch t-test #> - Alternative: Mann-Whitney U test #> - Reason: The data look like two independent groups with a continuous outcome and acceptable normality. #> - Next step: Run Welch t-test. Use the rank-based alternative if needed. #> - Notes: #>   * Normality was checked automatically and classified as `yes`.

id: 群間差の中での基本分岐
what: 群間差の中での基本分岐
title: 分岐で選ぶ
code: flowchart TD     A["Compare groups"] --> B{"Outcome type"}     B --> C["Continuous"]     B --> D["Binary or nominal"]     B --> E["Ordinal"]     B --> F["Count"]      C --> C1{"How many groups?"}     C1 -->|2| C2{"Paired?"}     C1 -->|3 or more| C3{"Repeated?"}     C2 -->|No| C4{"Normality?"}     C2 -->|Yes| C5{"Normality of paired difference?"}     C4 -->|Yes| T1["Welch t-test"]     C4 -->|No| T2["Mann-Whitney U test"]     C5 -->|Yes| T3["Paired t-test"]     C5 -->|No| T4["Wilcoxon signed-rank test"]     C3 -->|No| C6{"Normality?"}     C3 -->|Yes| C7{"Continuous repeated data?"}     C6 -->|Yes| T5["Welch ANOVA or ANOVA"]     C6 -->|No| T6["Kruskal-Wallis test"]     C7 -->|Yes| T7["Repeated-measures ANOVA"]     C7 -->|No| T8["Friedman test"]      D --> D1{"Paired?"}     D1 -->|No| D2{"Expected counts small?"}     D1 -->|Yes| T9["McNemar test"]     D2 -->|Yes| T10["Fisher exact test"]     D2 -->|No| T11["Chi-squared test"]      E --> E1{"Paired or repeated?"}     E1 -->|Independent 2 groups| T12["Mann-Whitney U test"]     E1 -->|Paired 2 groups| T13["Wilcoxon signed-rank test"]     E1 -->|Independent 3+ groups| T14["Kruskal-Wallis test"]     E1 -->|Repeated 3+ groups| T15["Friedman test"]      F --> T16["Redirect to count regression"]

id: どんなときに止めるか
what: どんなときに止めるか
title: 分岐で選ぶ
text: statsguider は、無理に単純検定へ進めないようにしています。 次のような場合は、推奨ではなく停止や別枝への案内を返します。 共変量で調整したい 生存時間を扱いたい 一致度をみたい 等価性や非劣性を示したい カウントデータを単純検定だけで扱おうとしている

id: 実際の操作はもっとシンプル
what: 実際の操作はもっとシンプル
title: 分岐で選ぶ
text: 分岐図の全体を覚える必要はありません。 実際には、次のように選択式の引数を入れるだけです。 必要な情報だけを入れれば、statsguider がその分岐に沿って手法を案内します。
code: select_test(   data = dat,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   run = "recommend" )

id: シナリオ1-独立2群の連続値
what: シナリオ1: 独立2群の連続値
title: シナリオ別チュートリアル
code: tbl_continuous <- data.frame(   group = c(rep("control", 6), rep("treated", 6)),   biomarker = c(10.2, 10.4, 10.1, 10.5, 10.3, 10.0, 11.1, 11.4, 11.0, 11.3, 11.5, 11.2) )  tbl_continuous #>      group biomarker #> 1  control      10.2 #> 2  control      10.4 #> 3  control      10.1 #> 4  control      10.5 #> 5  control      10.3 #> 6  control      10.0 #> 7  treated      11.1 #> 8  treated      11.4 #> 9  treated      11.0 #> 10 treated      11.3 #> 11 treated      11.5 #> 12 treated      11.2 select_test(   data = tbl_continuous,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   run = "recommend",   language = "ja" ) #> statsguider の案内 #> - 動作: 推奨 #> - 推奨手法: Welchのt検定 #> - 代替手法: Mann-Whitney U検定 #> - 理由: 独立2群の連続アウトカムで正規性も大きくは外れていないと判断しました。 #> - 次の一歩: Welchのt検定に進みます。必要なら順位ベースの代替も検討してください。 #> - 補足: #>   * 正規性を自動判定し、`yes` と分類しました。 run_test(   data = tbl_continuous,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   language = "ja" ) #> statsguider の結果 #> - 手法: Welchのt検定 #> - 理由: 独立2群の連続アウトカムで正規性も大きくは外れていないと判断しました。 #> - 要約: Welchのt検定 が選ばれた理由は、データが continuous 型、群数 2、paired = no、repeated = no と判定されたためです。

id: シナリオ2-対応ありの前後比較
what: シナリオ2: 対応ありの前後比較
title: シナリオ別チュートリアル
code: tbl_paired <- data.frame(   id = rep(paste0("S", 1:6), each = 2),   visit = rep(c("before", "after"), times = 6),   value = c(8.2, 9.0, 7.9, 8.8, 8.5, 9.1, 8.1, 8.9, 8.3, 9.0, 8.0, 8.7) )  tbl_paired #>    id  visit value #> 1  S1 before   8.2 #> 2  S1  after   9.0 #> 3  S2 before   7.9 #> 4  S2  after   8.8 #> 5  S3 before   8.5 #> 6  S3  after   9.1 #> 7  S4 before   8.1 #> 8  S4  after   8.9 #> 9  S5 before   8.3 #> 10 S5  after   9.0 #> 11 S6 before   8.0 #> 12 S6  after   8.7 select_test(   data = tbl_paired,   outcome = "value",   group = "visit",   id = "id",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "yes",   repeated = "no",   run = "recommend",   language = "ja" ) #> statsguider の案内 #> - 動作: 推奨 #> - 推奨手法: 対応のあるt検定 #> - 代替手法: Wilcoxon符号順位検定 #> - 理由: 対応のある2時点の連続アウトカムで正規性も大きくは外れていないと判断しました。 #> - 次の一歩: 対応のあるt検定に進みます。 #> - 補足: #>   * 正規性を自動判定し、`yes` と分類しました。

id: シナリオ3-独立3群の連続値
what: シナリオ3: 独立3群の連続値
title: シナリオ別チュートリアル
code: tbl_three_groups <- data.frame(   group = rep(c("control", "drugA", "drugB"), each = 5),   biomarker = c(     9.8, 10.1, 10.0, 9.9, 10.2,     10.8, 10.9, 11.1, 10.7, 11.0,     11.4, 11.2, 11.5, 11.3, 11.6   ) )  tbl_three_groups #>      group biomarker #> 1  control       9.8 #> 2  control      10.1 #> 3  control      10.0 #> 4  control       9.9 #> 5  control      10.2 #> 6    drugA      10.8 #> 7    drugA      10.9 #> 8    drugA      11.1 #> 9    drugA      10.7 #> 10   drugA      11.0 #> 11   drugB      11.4 #> 12   drugB      11.2 #> 13   drugB      11.5 #> 14   drugB      11.3 #> 15   drugB      11.6 select_test(   data = tbl_three_groups,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   run = "recommend",   language = "ja" ) #> statsguider の案内 #> - 動作: 推奨 #> - 推奨手法: Welch ANOVA #> - 代替手法: Kruskal-Wallis検定 #> - 理由: 3群以上の独立した連続アウトカムで正規性も大きくは外れていないと判断しました。 #> - 次の一歩: Welch ANOVAに進みます。 #> - 補足: #>   * 正規性を自動判定し、`yes` と分類しました。

id: シナリオ4-小標本のカテゴリデータ
what: シナリオ4: 小標本のカテゴリデータ
title: シナリオ別チュートリアル
code: tbl_small_cat <- data.frame(   group = c(rep("control", 6), rep("treated", 6)),   response = c("yes", "no", "no", "no", "no", "no", "yes", "yes", "no", "no", "no", "no") )  tbl_small_cat #>      group response #> 1  control      yes #> 2  control       no #> 3  control       no #> 4  control       no #> 5  control       no #> 6  control       no #> 7  treated      yes #> 8  treated      yes #> 9  treated       no #> 10 treated       no #> 11 treated       no #> 12 treated       no select_test(   data = tbl_small_cat,   outcome = "response",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "binary",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   run = "recommend",   language = "ja" ) #> statsguider の案内 #> - 動作: 推奨 #> - 推奨手法: Fisher正確確率検定 #> - 代替手法: χ²検定 #> - 理由: 独立2群のカテゴリデータで期待度数が小さいセルがあります。 #> - 次の一歩: Fisher正確確率検定に進みます。

id: シナリオ5-対応ありの二値データ
what: シナリオ5: 対応ありの二値データ
title: シナリオ別チュートリアル
code: tbl_paired_binary <- data.frame(   id = rep(paste0("P", 1:8), each = 2),   visit = rep(c("before", "after"), times = 8),   response = c("no", "yes", "no", "no", "yes", "yes", "no", "yes",                "yes", "yes", "no", "yes", "no", "no", "yes", "yes") )  tbl_paired_binary #>    id  visit response #> 1  P1 before       no #> 2  P1  after      yes #> 3  P2 before       no #> 4  P2  after       no #> 5  P3 before      yes #> 6  P3  after      yes #> 7  P4 before       no #> 8  P4  after      yes #> 9  P5 before      yes #> 10 P5  after      yes #> 11 P6 before       no #> 12 P6  after      yes #> 13 P7 before       no #> 14 P7  after       no #> 15 P8 before      yes #> 16 P8  after      yes select_test(   data = tbl_paired_binary,   outcome = "response",   group = "visit",   id = "id",   outcome_type = "binary",   paired = "yes",   repeated = "no",   run = "recommend",   language = "ja" ) #> statsguider の案内 #> - 動作: 推奨 #> - 推奨手法: McNemar検定 #> - 理由: 対応のある二値データと判断しました。 #> - 次の一歩: McNemar検定に進みます。

id: シナリオ6-反復測定の順序データ
what: シナリオ6: 反復測定の順序データ
title: シナリオ別チュートリアル
code: tbl_repeated_ordinal <- data.frame(   id = rep(paste0("S", 1:5), each = 3),   visit = rep(c("baseline", "week2", "week4"), times = 5),   score = ordered(     c(       "high", "medium", "low",       "medium", "medium", "low",       "high", "medium", "medium",       "medium", "low", "low",       "high", "medium", "low"     ),     levels = c("low", "medium", "high")   ) )  tbl_repeated_ordinal #>    id    visit  score #> 1  S1 baseline   high #> 2  S1    week2 medium #> 3  S1    week4    low #> 4  S2 baseline medium #> 5  S2    week2 medium #> 6  S2    week4    low #> 7  S3 baseline   high #> 8  S3    week2 medium #> 9  S3    week4 medium #> 10 S4 baseline medium #> 11 S4    week2    low #> 12 S4    week4    low #> 13 S5 baseline   high #> 14 S5    week2 medium #> 15 S5    week4    low select_test(   data = tbl_repeated_ordinal,   outcome = "score",   group = "visit",   id = "id",   outcome_type = "ordinal",   paired = "no",   repeated = "yes",   run = "recommend",   language = "ja" ) #> statsguider の案内 #> - 動作: 推奨 #> - 推奨手法: Friedman検定 #> - 代替手法: 反復測定ANOVA #> - 理由: 反復測定の順序データと判断しました。 #> - 次の一歩: Friedman検定に進みます。

id: シナリオ7-共変量調整が必要で別枝に進むケース
what: シナリオ7: 共変量調整が必要で別枝に進むケース
title: シナリオ別チュートリアル
code: tbl_adjust <- data.frame(   group = c(rep("control", 6), rep("treated", 6)),   age = c(41, 45, 39, 48, 42, 44, 37, 40, 38, 43, 39, 41),   biomarker = c(10.2, 10.4, 10.1, 10.5, 10.3, 10.0, 11.1, 11.4, 11.0, 11.3, 11.5, 11.2) )  tbl_adjust #>      group age biomarker #> 1  control  41      10.2 #> 2  control  45      10.4 #> 3  control  39      10.1 #> 4  control  48      10.5 #> 5  control  42      10.3 #> 6  control  44      10.0 #> 7  treated  37      11.1 #> 8  treated  40      11.4 #> 9  treated  38      11.0 #> 10 treated  43      11.3 #> 11 treated  39      11.5 #> 12 treated  41      11.2 select_test(   data = tbl_adjust,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   adjust = "yes",   run = "recommend",   language = "ja" ) #> statsguider の案内 #> - 動作: 別枝へ案内 #> - 理由: 調整が必要なので単純な非調整検定は適切な主枝ではありません。 #> - 次の一歩: 回帰または混合モデルの枝に進んでください。 #> - 補足: #>   * 共変量調整が必要なので、単純な検定よりモデル解析の方が適切です。 #>   * 正規性を自動判定し、`yes` と分類しました。

id: シナリオ8-カウントデータなので別枝に進むケース
what: シナリオ8: カウントデータなので別枝に進むケース
title: シナリオ別チュートリアル
text: このように、statsguider は「使うべき手法を出す」だけでなく、「単純検定では進まない方がよい」ケースも案内します。
code: tbl_count <- data.frame(   group = c(rep("control", 6), rep("treated", 6)),   colonies = c(12, 10, 11, 14, 9, 13, 18, 17, 20, 16, 19, 18) )  tbl_count #>      group colonies #> 1  control       12 #> 2  control       10 #> 3  control       11 #> 4  control       14 #> 5  control        9 #> 6  control       13 #> 7  treated       18 #> 8  treated       17 #> 9  treated       20 #> 10 treated       16 #> 11 treated       19 #> 12 treated       18 select_test(   data = tbl_count,   outcome = "colonies",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "count",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   run = "recommend",   language = "ja" ) #> statsguider の案内 #> - 動作: 別枝へ案内 #> - 理由: カウントアウトカムは単純比較検定に無理に入れるべきではありません。 #> - 次の一歩: Poisson回帰または負の二項回帰に進んでください。 #> - 補足: #>   * カウントアウトカムは通常、Poisson 回帰または負の二項回帰に進むべきです。

title: 主な関数
text: select_test() は、いちばん基本になる関数です。 使う場面: どの列を使うか分かっている データの性質を引数で指定したい 推奨だけ見たい、またはそのまま実行したい

title: 主な関数
text: 解析に使う data.frame 結果の列名 群や条件の列名 対応ありや反復測定のときに使う個体 ID 列名

title: 主な関数
text: "difference": 群間差をみたい "association": 関連をみたい "adjusted_effect": 調整した効果をみたい "time_to_event": 生存時間をみたい "agreement": 一致度をみたい "equivalence": 等価性や非劣性をみたい "continuous": 連続値 "binary": 二値 "nominal": 名義カテゴリ "ordinal": 順序カテゴリ "count": カウント "yes": 同じ個体を 2 回比べる "": 独立した群を比べる "yes": 同じ個体を 3 回以上測る "": 反復測定ではない "yes": 共変量調整が必要 "": 単純比較でよい "auto": 自動で参考判定 "yes": 正規性を仮定する "": 正規性を仮定しない "unknown": わからない "recommend": 推奨手法だけを見る "run": 推奨手法を実行する "en": 英語表示 "ja": 日本語表示

title: 主な関数
code: tbl_select <- data.frame(   group = c(rep("control", 6), rep("treated", 6)),   biomarker = c(10.2, 10.4, 10.1, 10.5, 10.3, 10.0, 11.1, 11.4, 11.0, 11.3, 11.5, 11.2) )  select_test(   data = tbl_select,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   run = "recommend",   language = "ja" ) #> statsguider の案内 #> - 動作: 推奨 #> - 推奨手法: Welchのt検定 #> - 代替手法: Mann-Whitney U検定 #> - 理由: 独立2群の連続アウトカムで正規性も大きくは外れていないと判断しました。 #> - 次の一歩: Welchのt検定に進みます。必要なら順位ベースの代替も検討してください。 #> - 補足: #>   * 正規性を自動判定し、`yes` と分類しました。

title: 主な関数
text: outcome は「比較したい値」の列です group は「control と treated のような群」の列です 同じ個体を前後で測ったなら paired = "yes" です 同じ個体を 3 時点以上測ったなら repeated = "yes" です 年齢や性別などで調整したいなら adjust = "yes" です まず結果を確認したいだけなら run = "recommend" にします

title: 主な関数
code: select_test(   data = tbl_select,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   run = "run",   language = "ja" ) #> statsguider の結果 #> - 手法: Welchのt検定 #> - 理由: 独立2群の連続アウトカムで正規性も大きくは外れていないと判断しました。 #> - 要約: Welchのt検定 が選ばれた理由は、データが continuous 型、群数 2、paired = no、repeated = no と判定されたためです。

title: 主な関数
text: guided_test() は、質問に答える形で分岐をたどりたいときに使います。 使う場面: 引数を一度に書きたくない 何を選べばよいか確認しながら進みたい 半自動ナビゲーションとして使いたい

title: 主な関数
text: guided_test() では、対話的に答える代わりに answers = list(...) で値を渡せます。 代表的な項目: goal outcome group id paired repeated adjust outcome_type normality

title: 主な関数
code: guided_test(   tbl_select,   answers = list(     goal = "difference",     outcome = "biomarker",     group = "group",     paired = "no",     repeated = "no",     adjust = "no",     outcome_type = "continuous",     normality = "auto"   ),   run = "recommend",   language = "ja" ) #> statsguider の案内 #> - 動作: 推奨 #> - 推奨手法: Welchのt検定 #> - 代替手法: Mann-Whitney U検定 #> - 理由: 独立2群の連続アウトカムで正規性も大きくは外れていないと判断しました。 #> - 次の一歩: Welchのt検定に進みます。必要なら順位ベースの代替も検討してください。 #> - 補足: #>   * 正規性を自動判定し、`yes` と分類しました。

title: 主な関数
text: select_test() は、引数をまとめて直接指定する関数です guided_test() は、分岐を一つずつ確認しながら進む関数です

title: 主な関数
text: recommend_test() は、推奨手法だけを見たいときに使います。 返ってくる内容: action recommended method alternative method reason next step notes

title: 主な関数
code: recommend_test(   data = tbl_select,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   language = "ja" ) #> statsguider の案内 #> - 動作: 推奨 #> - 推奨手法: Welchのt検定 #> - 代替手法: Mann-Whitney U検定 #> - 理由: 独立2群の連続アウトカムで正規性も大きくは外れていないと判断しました。 #> - 次の一歩: Welchのt検定に進みます。必要なら順位ベースの代替も検討してください。 #> - 補足: #>   * 正規性を自動判定し、`yes` と分類しました。

title: 主な関数
text: 実行前に選ばれた手法を確認したい 自分の理解と一致しているかを見たい レポートや説明の前に分岐だけ確認したい

title: 主な関数
text: run_test() は、推奨された手法をそのまま実行したいときに使います。 この関数は、最初に内部で推奨を確認してから実行します。 そのため、不適切な分岐なら無理に実行せず、停止や別枝への案内になります。

title: 主な関数
code: run_test(   data = tbl_select,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   language = "ja" ) #> statsguider の結果 #> - 手法: Welchのt検定 #> - 理由: 独立2群の連続アウトカムで正規性も大きくは外れていないと判断しました。 #> - 要約: Welchのt検定 が選ばれた理由は、データが continuous 型、群数 2、paired = no、repeated = no と判定されたためです。

title: 主な関数
text: 推奨だけでなく検定結果までほしい 不適切な解析を避けながら実行したい

title: 主な関数
text: check_design() は、解析前に設計が適切かどうかを確認したいときに使います。 チェックする内容: 必要な列があるか 群数が足りているか 対応ありや反復測定なのに id が抜けていないか 共変量調整が必要なのに単純検定へ進もうとしていないか

title: 主な関数
code: check_design(   data = tbl_select,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   adjust = "no",   language = "ja" ) #> $ok #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $issues #> character(0) #>  #> $warnings #> character(0) #>  #> $inputs #> $inputs$goal #> [1] "difference" #>  #> $inputs$outcome_type #> [1] "continuous" #>  #> $inputs$group_count #> [1] "2" #>  #> $inputs$paired #> [1] "no" #>  #> $inputs$repeated #> [1] "no" #>  #> $inputs$adjust #> [1] "no"

title: 主な関数
text: まず設計の整合性だけ見たい 実行前に不足情報がないか確認したい

title: 主な関数
text: select_test(): もっとも基本で、最初に使う関数 guided_test(): 質問に答えながら選びたいときに使う関数 recommend_test(): 推奨手法だけを確認したいときに使う関数 run_test(): 推奨手法を実行したいときに使う関数 check_design(): 設計の妥当性を確認したいときに使う関数

title: はじめに
text: 最初に覚える関数は select_test() です。 最低限必要なのは次の列指定です。 data: 解析する data.frame outcome: 結果の列 group: 群や条件の列 id: 対応ありや反復測定のときの個体 ID 列 列指定以外は、基本的に選択式の引数です。 "difference": 群間差をみたい "association": 関連をみたい "adjusted_effect": 調整した効果をみたい "time_to_event": 生存時間をみたい "agreement": 一致度をみたい "equivalence": 等価性や非劣性をみたい "continuous": 連続値 "binary": 二値 "nominal": 名義カテゴリ "ordinal": 順序カテゴリ "count": カウント "yes": 同じ個体を 2 回比べる "": 独立した群を比べる "yes": 同じ個体を 3 回以上測る "": 反復測定ではない "yes": 共変量調整が必要 "": 単純比較でよい "auto": 自動で参考判定する "yes": 正規性を仮定する "": 正規性を仮定しない "unknown": わからない "recommend": 推奨手法だけを返す "run": 推奨手法をそのまま実行する "en": 英語表示 "ja": 日本語表示

id: まずは推奨だけを見る
what: まずは推奨だけを見る
title: はじめに
text: この例では、独立 2 群の連続値なので、まず推奨手法が返ります。
code: dat <- subset(statsguider::wet_example, visit == "week4")  select_test(   data = dat,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   run = "recommend",   language = "ja" ) #> statsguider の案内 #> - 動作: 推奨 #> - 推奨手法: Welchのt検定 #> - 代替手法: Mann-Whitney U検定 #> - 理由: 独立2群の連続アウトカムで正規性も大きくは外れていないと判断しました。 #> - 次の一歩: Welchのt検定に進みます。必要なら順位ベースの代替も検討してください。 #> - 補足: #>   * 正規性を自動判定し、`yes` と分類しました。

title: はじめに
text: 解析まで進めたいときは run = "run" にします。
code: select_test(   data = dat,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   run = "run",   language = "ja" ) #> statsguider の結果 #> - 手法: Welchのt検定 #> - 理由: 独立2群の連続アウトカムで正規性も大きくは外れていないと判断しました。 #> - 要約: Welchのt検定 が選ばれた理由は、データが continuous 型、群数 2、paired = no、repeated = no と判定されたためです。

id: 質問に答えながら進める
what: 質問に答えながら進める
title: はじめに
text: guided_test() は、分岐を一つずつ確認したいときに向いています。
code: guided_test(   dat,   answers = list(     goal = "difference",     outcome = "biomarker",     group = "group",     paired = "no",     repeated = "no",     adjust = "no",     outcome_type = "continuous",     normality = "auto"   ),   run = "recommend",   language = "ja" ) #> statsguider の案内 #> - 動作: 推奨 #> - 推奨手法: Welchのt検定 #> - 代替手法: Mann-Whitney U検定 #> - 理由: 独立2群の連続アウトカムで正規性も大きくは外れていないと判断しました。 #> - 次の一歩: Welchのt検定に進みます。必要なら順位ベースの代替も検討してください。 #> - 補足: #>   * 正規性を自動判定し、`yes` と分類しました。

title: はじめに
text: select_test() を中心に知りたいとき: 「主な関数」 いろいろなデータ表で試したいとき: 「シナリオ別チュートリアル」 分岐の考え方を見たいとき: 「分岐で選ぶ」

title: Authors and Citation
text: Dai Dai. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Dai D (2026). statsguider: Semi-Automatic Guidance Statistical Test Selection. R package version 1.0.0, https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {statsguider: Semi-Automatic Guidance for Statistical Test Selection},   author = {Dai Dai},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.0.0},   url = {https://dai540.github.io/statsguider/}, }

title: statsguider
text: Simple statistical test selection data.frame. statsguider helps choose statistical method branching data properties, run recommended method.

title: statsguider
code: install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("dai540/statsguider") library(statsguider)

title: statsguider
code: dat <- subset(wet_example, visit == "week4")  select_test(   data = dat,   outcome = "biomarker",   group = "group",   outcome_type = "continuous",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   run = "run" )

title: statsguider
text: Start Choose branching Scenario tutorials Main functions はじめに 分岐で選ぶ シナリオ別チュートリアル 主な関数

title: statsguider
text: tutorial creates small data table tutorial applies select_test() run_test() scenario shows set branching arguments 各チュートリアルで小さなデータ表を自分で作ります そのデータ表に select_test() や run_test() を適用します 分岐に必要な引数をどう設定するかも一緒に示します

what: Check Whether a Design Fits the Simple Branching Workflow — check_design
title: Check Whether a Design Fits the Simple Branching Workflow — check_design
text: Performs structural validation recommending running statistical test. function intentionally conservative redirects designs forced simple test.

title: Check Whether a Design Fits the Simple Branching Workflow — check_design
code: check_design(   data,   outcome,   group = NULL,   id = NULL,   goal = "difference",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   adjust = "no",   outcome_type = NULL,   language = "en" )

title: Check Whether a Design Fits the Simple Branching Workflow — check_design
text: data data frame. outcome Name outcome column. group Optional name group column. id Optional subject identifier column paired repeated designs. goal Analysis goal. One "difference", "association", "adjusted_effect", "time_to_event", "agreement", "equivalence". paired "yes" "". repeated "yes" "". adjust "yes" "". outcome_type Optional outcome type. One "continuous", "binary", "nominal", "ordinal", "count". language "en" "ja".

title: Check Whether a Design Fits the Simple Branching Workflow — check_design
text: list ok, issues, warnings, inputs.

what: Guide a User Through Simple Test Selection — guided_test
title: Guide a User Through Simple Test Selection — guided_test
text: Starts simple branching workflow. interactive sessions function asks small number questions. scripts, answers can supplied named list.

title: Guide a User Through Simple Test Selection — guided_test
code: guided_test(data, answers = NULL, run = "recommend", language = "en")

title: Guide a User Through Simple Test Selection — guided_test
text: data data frame. answers Optional named list answers non-interactive use. run One "recommend" "run". language "en" "ja".

title: Guide a User Through Simple Test Selection — guided_test
text: statsguider_decision object, statsguider_result object run = "run".

what: Recommend a Statistical Test from Simple Data Properties — recommend_test
title: Recommend a Statistical Test from Simple Data Properties — recommend_test
text: Uses rule-table driven engine recommend statistical test, request information, stop inappropriate branch.

title: Recommend a Statistical Test from Simple Data Properties — recommend_test
code: recommend_test(   data,   outcome,   group = NULL,   id = NULL,   goal = "difference",   paired = "no",   repeated = "no",   adjust = "no",   outcome_type = NULL,   normality = "auto",   language = "en" )

title: Recommend a Statistical Test from Simple Data Properties — recommend_test
text: data data frame. outcome Name outcome column. group Optional name group column. id Optional subject identifier column. goal Analysis goal. One "difference", "association", "adjusted_effect", "time_to_event", "agreement", "equivalence". paired "yes" "". repeated "yes" "". adjust "yes" "". outcome_type Optional manual outcome type. title: statsguider 1.0.0
text: Initial release statsguider. Added rule-table driven recommender common group-comparison workflows. Added guarded execution common base R tests. Added interactive guided mode, example data, README, vignettes, pkgdown site configuration.
